title: Private Fund Advisory Fee Review
content:
CONFIDENTIAL MEMORANDUM

TO: Private Wealth Advisory Team
FROM: Compliance Department
DATE: March 15, 2025
RE: Private Fund Advisory Fee Review - Q1 2025

This memorandum addresses the quarterly review of advisory fee structures for alternative investment funds under management, in compliance with AIFMD Article 22 disclosure requirements and MiFID II Article 24 client best interest obligations.

FEE STRUCTURE ANALYSIS

Our current private fund advisory fee model ranges from 0.85% to 1.65% annually on committed capital, with performance fees between 15-20% above the relevant hurdle rates. Following our comprehensive review, we identified several adjustments required for optimal client alignment and regulatory compliance.

For our flagship European Private Equity Fund III, the management fee has been restructured from 2.0% to 1.75% on committed capital during the investment period, declining to 1.25% on cost basis thereafter. This adjustment affects approximately €285 million in committed capital across 47 institutional investors.

CLIENT IMPACT ASSESSMENT

Significant fee impacts have been identified for ultra-high-net-worth clients with alternative allocations exceeding €5 million. Client Robert J. Williams (Account #4821-7734) exemplifies this category with €12.3 million across four private fund strategies. His annual advisory fees have decreased by 23 basis points to 1.42%, resulting in cost savings of approximately €28,300 annually.

Our Real Estate Opportunity Fund II has implemented a more sophisticated fee waterfall structure. The catch-up provision now applies only after investors receive an 8% preferred return, compared to the previous 6% threshold. This modification directly benefits our private wealth clients who represent 34% of fund commitments.

REGULATORY COMPLIANCE UPDATES

Under AIFMD Article 22, we have enhanced our periodic reporting to include granular fee breakdowns and performance attribution analysis. All private fund investors now receive quarterly statements detailing management fees, performance allocations, and expense ratios with improved transparency metrics.

MiFID II Article 24 compliance has been strengthened through our enhanced suitability assessment framework. We now conduct semi-annual reviews of client alternative investment allocations, ensuring continued alignment with risk tolerance and investment objectives. This process includes stress testing scenarios and liquidity planning assessments.

OPERATIONAL CHANGES

Effective April 1, 2025, all private fund fee calculations will be processed through our upgraded Portfolio Management System (PMS). This enhancement provides real-time fee accruals and improved reconciliation capabilities. The system automatically flags any fee calculations exceeding predetermined thresholds for manual review.

For questions regarding specific client accounts or fee calculations, contact the Private Wealth Services desk at +44 (0) 207 555-0147 during London market hours.

NEXT STEPS

All Private Wealth Advisors must complete the updated Alternative Investment Fee Training module by March 31, 2025. This mandatory training covers the new fee structures, regulatory requirements, and client communication protocols. Completion certificates must be submitted to the Compliance Department for record-keeping.

The next quarterly fee review is scheduled for June 15, 2025, with particular focus on performance fee accruals and carried interest calculations for funds approaching their distribution phases.

This memorandum contains confidential and proprietary information of Meridian Wealth Partners. Distribution is restricted to authorized personnel with appropriate clearance levels.
